---
title: "scimago_journals"
author: "Josefina Weinerova"
date: "2025-08-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# package loading
if (!require(pacman)) install.packages("pacman")

pacman::p_load(
  openalexR, cowplot, tidyverse, httr, jsonlite,
  citecorp, rcrossref, purrr, dplyr, tibble, fect, furrr, future, data.table,
  panelView,sjrdata,openxlsx
)
```
#load relevant datasets
```{r}

df_meta <- readRDS("data/metadata_OpenAlex.rds")
df_journals <- sjr_journals
```

# prepare the datasets for matching 
```{r}

df_journals_L <- df_journals %>%
  separate_rows(issn, sep = "\\s*,\\s*") %>%  # split on comma and space
  mutate(issn = str_trim(issn))
#write.xlsx(df_journals_L,"df_journals_L.xlsx")

## for metadata from OA output
df_meta <- df_meta %>% mutate(issn = str_replace_all(issn_l, "-", ""))

# merge - selecting the journals that the original studies have been published in
journals_orig <- merge(
  df_meta, df_journals_L,
  by = "issn",
  all.x = TRUE,
  suffixes = c("_s", "")   
)

# did I lose any dois?
setdiff(df_meta$doi_o,journals_orig$doi_o)
#match journals in the same year or in the closest year
# exact matches per work (keep all of them)
exacts <- journals_orig %>%
  group_by(id) %>%                 # or group_by(doi)
  filter(publication_year == year) %>%
  ungroup()

# for works with no exact match, keep the closest year (average if multiple equidistant)
closest <- journals_orig %>%
  filter(!id %in% exacts$id) %>%   
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(diff = abs(publication_year - year)) %>%
  filter(diff == min(diff)) %>%
  mutate(sjr = mean(sjr)) %>%
  select(-diff) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3) combine
journals_best <- bind_rows(exacts, closest)
setdiff(df_meta$doi_o,journals_best$doi_o) #checking that all dois that are in the meta df are also in the journals_best

```
# cleaning
```{r}
journals_orig<- journals_best
#cleaning the datasets
df_journals$categories <- str_replace_all(df_journals$categories, "\\s*\\(Q\\d+\\)", "")
journals_orig$categories <- str_replace_all(journals_orig$categories, "\\s*\\(Q\\d+\\)", "")

```


```{r}
# If SJR is missing for the target journal-year, keep it missing:
# those targets will only retain the original journal-year as control.
journals_orig %>% filter(is.na(sjr))
```

# Function to find closest journals by category and sjr

```{r}
#divide categories from one column into column per category
K <- df_journals %>%
  summarise(max_k = max(str_count(coalesce(categories, ""), ";"), na.rm = TRUE) + 1) %>%
  pull(max_k)
K2 <- journals_orig  %>%
  summarise(max_k = max(str_count(coalesce(categories, ""), ";"), na.rm = TRUE) + 1) %>%
  pull(max_k)
K_all <- max(K, K2)

df_journals <- df_journals %>%
  separate(
    categories,
    into = paste0("cat_", seq_len(K_all)),
    sep = "\\s*;\\s*",   # split on ';' and trim spaces
    fill = "right",      # pad with NAs to the right
    remove = TRUE)

journals_orig <- journals_orig %>%
  separate(
    categories,
    into = paste0("cat_", seq_len(K_all)),
    sep = "\\s*;\\s*",   # split on ';' and trim spaces
    fill = "right",      # pad with NAs to the right
    remove = TRUE)
#for a journal find other journals that match one of the categories and sjr range and year
# Identify category columns dynamically
cat_cols <- paste0("cat_", seq_len(K_all))

#--- 1) Targets (O): one row per DOI-year, long by category token
O_long <- journals_orig %>%
  mutate(
    sjr_target = as.numeric(sjr),
    sjr_min    = sjr_target * 0.75,
    sjr_max    = sjr_target * 1.25
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(doi_o, year, sjr_target, sjr_min, sjr_max, all_of(cat_cols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(cat_cols), names_to = "cat", values_to = "token") %>%
  filter(!is.na(token), token != "") %>%
  mutate(token = str_trim(token)) %>%
  distinct(doi_o, year, sjr_target, sjr_min, sjr_max, token)   # de-dup tokens per DOI

#--- 2) Pool (J): one row per journal-year, long by category token
J_long <- df_journals %>%
  mutate(sjr_pool = as.numeric(sjr)) %>%
  select(sourceid, year, sjr_pool, all_of(cat_cols)) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(cat_cols), names_to = "cat", values_to = "token") %>%
  filter(!is.na(token), token != "") %>%
  mutate(token = str_trim(token)) %>%
  distinct(sourceid, year, sjr_pool, token)                 # de-dup tokens per journal

#--- 3) Seed with original journal (always included)
seed_orig <- journals_orig %>%
  transmute(
    doi_o, year, sourceid,
    overlap = NA_integer_,
    tokens = NA_character_,
    sjr_target = as.numeric(sjr),
    sjr_pool = as.numeric(sjr),
    sjr_diff = 0,
    sjr, title, issn, across(all_of(cat_cols)),
    topup = FALSE,
    match_stage = "original",
    relax_step = NA_character_
  ) %>%
  distinct(doi_o, year, sourceid, .keep_all = TRUE)

#--- 4) Strict candidates (for non-missing target SJR only)
cand_strict <- O_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(sjr_target)) %>%
  inner_join(J_long, by = c("year", "token")) %>%
  filter(between(sjr_pool, sjr_min, sjr_max))

#--- 5) Collapse strict candidates to pair-level and compute overlap + closeness
pairs_strict <- cand_strict %>%
  dplyr::group_by(doi_o, sourceid, year) %>%
  summarise(
    overlap    = n_distinct(token),
    tokens     = paste(unique(token), collapse = "; "),# # of shared categories
    sjr_target = first(sjr_target),
    sjr_pool   = first(sjr_pool),
    sjr_diff   = abs(sjr_pool - sjr_target),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# strict matches ordered by overlap, then closeness
strict_add <- pairs_strict %>%
  group_by(doi_o, year) %>%
  arrange(desc(overlap), sjr_diff, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  distinct(sourceid, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 6) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(
    df_journals %>%
      select(sourceid, year, sjr, title, issn, all_of(cat_cols)),
    by = c("sourceid", "year")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topup = FALSE,
    match_stage = "strict",
    relax_step = NA_character_
  )

# combine and deduplicate: keep original row if the same sourceid-year appears
matched_pool <- bind_rows(seed_orig, strict_add) %>%
  distinct(doi_o, year, sourceid, .keep_all = TRUE)
```
#loose criteria for those that did not find a match
```{r}
all_keys <- journals_orig %>% distinct(doi_o, year)

compute_needs <- function(pool, all_keys, min_n = 3L) {
  all_keys %>%
    left_join(pool %>% count(doi_o, year, name = "n_rows"), by = c("doi_o", "year")) %>%
    mutate(
      n_rows = coalesce(n_rows, 0L),
      to_take = pmax(0L, min_n - n_rows)
    ) %>%
    filter(to_take > 0L)
}

needs <- compute_needs(matched_pool, all_keys, min_n = 3L)

# progressively relax SJR window until reaching at least 3 matches where possible
relax_windows <- tibble(
  label = c("50%", "100%", "none"),
  window = c(0.50, 1.00, Inf)
)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(relax_windows))) {
  if (nrow(needs) == 0) break

  w <- relax_windows$window[i]
  lbl <- relax_windows$label[i]

  O_need <- O_long %>%
    semi_join(needs, by = c("doi_o", "year"))

  cand_relaxed <- O_need %>%
    inner_join(J_long, by = c("year", "token")) %>%
    # if target SJR is missing, keep only original seeded match
    filter(!is.na(sjr_target)) %>%
    anti_join(
      matched_pool %>% distinct(doi_o, year, sourceid),
      by = c("doi_o", "year", "sourceid")
    )

  if (is.finite(w)) {
    cand_relaxed <- cand_relaxed %>%
      filter(between(sjr_pool, sjr_target * (1 - w), sjr_target * (1 + w)))
  }

  if (nrow(cand_relaxed) == 0) {
    next
  }

  pairs_relaxed <- cand_relaxed %>%
    group_by(doi_o, sourceid, year) %>%
    summarise(
      overlap    = n_distinct(token),
      tokens     = paste(unique(token), collapse = "; "),
      sjr_target = first(sjr_target),
      sjr_pool   = first(sjr_pool),
      sjr_diff   = abs(sjr_pool - sjr_target),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  add_these <- pairs_relaxed %>%
    inner_join(needs %>% select(doi_o, year, to_take), by = c("doi_o", "year")) %>%
    group_by(doi_o, year) %>%
    arrange(sjr_diff, desc(overlap), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    distinct(sourceid, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number()) %>%
    filter(rank <= first(to_take)) %>%
    select(-rank, -to_take) %>%
    ungroup()

  add_these_full <- add_these %>%
    left_join(
      df_journals %>% select(sourceid, year, sjr, title, issn, all_of(cat_cols)),
      by = c("sourceid", "year")
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      topup = TRUE,
      match_stage = "relaxed",
      relax_step = lbl
    )

  matched_pool <- bind_rows(matched_pool, add_these_full) %>%
    distinct(doi_o, year, sourceid, .keep_all = TRUE)

  needs <- compute_needs(matched_pool, all_keys, min_n = 3L)
}

if (nrow(needs) > 0) {
  message(sprintf("Still below 3 matches for %d doi-year pairs after all relaxation steps.", nrow(needs)))
}

# final per doi-year cap at 6 (keep original first, then strict, then progressively relaxed)
top6_full <- matched_pool %>%
  mutate(
    stage_priority = case_when(
      match_stage == "original" ~ 0L,
      match_stage == "strict" ~ 1L,
      relax_step == "50%" ~ 2L,
      relax_step == "100%" ~ 3L,
      relax_step == "none" ~ 4L,
      TRUE ~ 9L
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(doi_o, year) %>%
  arrange(stage_priority, desc(overlap), sjr_diff, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 6) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-stage_priority) %>%
  add_count(doi_o, year, name = "n_rows")
table(top6_full$n_rows)

#join also journals orig info
journals_orig$sourceid_orig <- journals_orig$sourceid
journals_orig$year_orig <- journals_orig$year
orig_data <- journals_orig %>% select(doi_o,sourceid,publication_year)
nrow(journals_orig)
matched_journals <- merge(top6_full,orig_data, by="doi_o")
matched_journals <- distinct(matched_journals)

table(matched_journals$n_rows)
head(matched_journals)
setdiff(df_meta$doi_o,matched_journals$doi_o)
matched_journals %>% filter(doi_o=="10.1007/s10802-020-00712-w")
```


#save output
```{r}
saveRDS(df_journals_L,"data/df_journals_L.rds")
saveRDS(matched_journals, "data/matched_journals_pilot.rds")

```
